---
title: "Quick Start Guide"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Quick Start Guide}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

## Overview

This vignette demonstrates how to use the `skinCellTypeAnnotation` pipeline to annotate your scRNA-seq data using the Reynolds 2021 human skin cell atlas reference.

## Installation

```r
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/lukishenning/skin-celltype-annotation.git

# Or install as R package (when available on CRAN/bioc)
# devtools::install_github("lukishenning/skin-celltype-annotation")
```

## Required Data

### Reference Data
Download the Reynolds 2021 reference from Zenodo:
- URL: https://zenodo.org/record/4569496
- File: `healthy.h5ad` (~1.2 GB)

### Query Data
Prepare your integrated Seurat object with:
- Normalized expression data
- Metadata including a `condition` column
- Properly formatted cell/gene names

## Usage

### Basic Usage

```r
library(Seurat)

# Load the annotation function
source("R/run_annotation.R")

# Run annotation
annotated <- run_reference_annotation(
  query_file = "path/to/your/query.rds",
  reference_file = "path/to/healthy.h5ad",
  output_dir = "path/to/output"
)
```

### With Custom Parameters

```r
annotated <- run_reference_annotation(
  query_file = "data/query_integrated.rds",
  reference_file = "data/reference/healthy.h5ad",
  output_dir = "results",
  n_hvg = 2000,      # Number of variable features
  n_pcs = 30,        # Number of PCA dimensions
  k_anchor = 20,     # Number of anchors to find
  k_score = 30       # Number of neighbors for scoring
)
```

## Output Files

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `seurat_annotated_reference.rds` | Your query object with cell type predictions |
| `reference_celltypes.pdf` | UMAP of the reference cell types |
| `reference_annotation_results.pdf` | Query UMAP with predictions + condition |
| `reference_annotation_by_condition.pdf` | Split UMAP by condition |
| `reference_celltype_composition.pdf` | Cell type proportions bar plots |
| `reference_celltype_composition.csv` | Raw composition data |
| `reference_prediction_violin.pdf` | Prediction confidence by cell type |
| `reference_dotplot.pdf` | Marker gene expression dotplot |

## Accessing Results

```r
# Load annotated object
annotated <- readRDS("results/seurat_annotated_reference.rds")

# View predicted cell types
table(annotated$predicted.id)

# View by condition
table(annotated$predicted.id, annotated$condition)

# View prediction confidence
summary(annotated$prediction.score.max)

# Find low confidence cells
low_confidence <- annotated[, annotated$prediction.score.max < 0.5]
table(low_confidence$predicted.id)
```

## Customizing Annotations

If you want to merge or rename cell types:

```r
# Merge similar cell types
annotated$celltype_custom <- annotated$predicted.id
annotated$celltype_custom <- gsub("F1|F2|F3", "Fibroblasts", annotated$celltype_custom)
annotated$celltype_custom <- gsub("Macro_1|Macro_2|Inf_mac", "Macrophages", annotated$celltype_custom)

# Re-plot
Seurat::DimPlot(annotated, group.by = "celltype_custom", label = TRUE)
```

## Troubleshooting

### Memory Issues
If you run out of memory:
- Reduce `n_hvg` to 1000
- Process samples in batches
- Use a machine with more RAM (64GB+ recommended)

### Low Prediction Scores
Cells with low confidence may:
- Be rare cell types not in reference
- Represent novel disease states
- Need additional QC filtering

### Session Info

```r
sessionInfo()
```
